prompt:
The theming engine needs one color module instead of the three half-broken helpers we copy-paste around. Please write color.ts with these exports — test_color.ts is the acceptance contract (`node --test test_color.ts`, don't edit it):

parseHex(input) -> { r, g, b, a }. r/g/b are 0-255 integers, a is 0..1. Accept 3-, 6- and 8-digit forms, hash optional, case-insensitive; 3-digit expands per CSS (#abc == #aabbcc); 8-digit carries alpha in the last byte. Anything else — wrong length, bad chars — throws TypeError.

toHex(color) -> lowercase '#rrggbb', or '#rrggbbaa' only when alpha < 1 (missing alpha means opaque). Out-of-range channels get CLAMPED to 0-255 (alpha to 0..1) and fractional channels rounded — designers feed us r: 300 and r: -20 all day, it must not throw.

rgbToHsl({ r, g, b }) -> { h, s, l } with h in [0, 360) degrees, s and l as 0-100 percentages. Achromatic colors report h: 0, s: 0. Don't round these — the tests allow small float tolerance.

hslToRgb({ h, s, l }) -> integer { r, g, b }. Hue wraps modulo 360 (negative too: -30 == 330), s and l clamp to 0-100.

mix(c1, c2, t) -> linear interpolation of r, g, b and a at t, with t clamped to [0,1] and channels rounded to integers. Colors without a mix as opaque.

Stdlib only, no npm.
